setting.note: Fifteen stories, fifteen corners of one city, each with a real address. From a boy's blind street in the north inner city to the aunts' house on the Liffey quays, Joyce mapped Dublin so exactly you can still walk it.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. James Joyce, first published 1914; the author died in 1941.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1941).
rights.attribution: James Joyce, Dubliners (1914). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The amber-yellow of the yellowed leaves the boy prizes in the dead priest's books, a colour drawn from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.
